name: Entrepeñas Reservoir and Casasana Port Loop from Buendía
name_original: Embalse de Entrepeñas y Puerto Casasana — circular desde Buendía
description: This difficult 53.6-mile road cycling route from Buendía offers panoramic views of the Embalse de Entrepeñas and challenging climbs.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Buendía traverse a varied landscape in the province of Cuenca, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain. The region is characterized by expansive pine forests, unique sandstone formations, and the significant presence of the Buendía reservoir and the Guadiela River. Road cyclists encounter terrain ranging from flat sections around the reservoir to more undulating paths through forests and hills, including routes that approach the Sierra de Altamira. This environment provides diverse options for road cycling, from moderate loops to more challenging climbs. Best road cycling routes around Buendía The most popular road cycling route is Entrepeñas Reservoir and Casasana Port Loop from Buendía, a 53.6 miles (86.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 17 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive views of the Entrepeñas Reservoir and challenging ascents. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Entrepeñas Viaduct – Entrepeñas Reservoir Dam loop from Estación de Auñón, a moderate 38.5 miles (61.9 km) path. This route features scenic stretches along the reservoir and passes over the impressive Entrepeñas Viaduct. Local road cyclists also love the Cifuentes River Waterfall – Entrepeñas Reservoir Dam loop from Estación de Auñón, a 77.3 miles (124.5 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including the Cifuentes River area and the Entrepeñas Reservoir, often completed in about 6 hours 18 minutes. Road cycling around Buendía is defined by reservoir views, pine forests, and sandstone formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult climbs. The routes in Buendía are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Buendía's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Buendía?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes around Buendía, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.0 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Buendía?
A: Road cycling in Buendía offers a diverse landscape. You'll find routes with flat sections around the reservoirs, undulating paths through expansive pine forests, and more challenging climbs as you approach areas like the Sierra de Altamira. The region is also characterized by unique sandstone formations.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or less experienced road cyclists?
A: Yes, Buendía offers a few easier road cycling routes. While many routes feature moderate to difficult climbs, there are options designed for those seeking a less strenuous ride, particularly around the flatter sections near the reservoir.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling in Buendía?
A: The routes often feature stunning views of the Buendía and Entrepeñas reservoirs, with their distinctive turquoise waters. You'll also cycle through thick pine forests and past unique sandstone formations. Notable viewpoints include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5398646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de la Virgen Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6503495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de los Borrachos Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas of the landscape.

Q: Are there any unique cultural attractions accessible by road bike?
A: Absolutely. The famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3905216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces&lt;/a&gt; (Ruta de las Caras) is a distinctive open-air museum with sculptures carved into sandstone rocks. While the main trail is a hiking path, you can cycle to its vicinity and explore the area. Another interesting spot is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5440120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Sacred Heart of Jesus of La Coronilla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Buendía?
A: Buendía is generally pleasant for road cycling during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be quite warm, especially in the midday sun, but early mornings or late afternoons offer cooler conditions. Winter can be chilly, but clear days are still suitable for riding.

Q: Is there parking available for road cyclists in Buendía?
A: Yes, Buendía has facilities like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7012012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Motorhome Park&lt;/a&gt;, which can serve as a starting point for rides and offers parking options. Parking is generally available in and around the village, making it convenient to access the routes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options?
A: While many routes involve significant climbs, some flatter sections around the Buendía reservoir are more suitable for families or those looking for a relaxed ride. It's advisable to check the elevation profiles of specific routes to ensure they match your family's ability.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: Buendía village itself offers cafes and restaurants. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3122364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Club Náutico Bolarque&lt;/a&gt; near the reservoir provides amenities. The area around the Ermita de la Virgen de los Desamparados, accessible via a scenic trail, also has picnic facilities, a fountain, and benches, perfect for a break.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Buendía offers several challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Dam – Entrepeñas Reservoir Dam loop from Almonacid de Zorita&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 95.6 km route with significant elevation gain, providing a demanding experience for seasoned cyclists.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Buendía?
A: Many of the road cycling routes around Buendía are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40382989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Viaduct – Entrepeñas Reservoir Dam loop from Estación de Auñón&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 61.9 km path that offers scenic stretches along the reservoir.

Q: What makes Buendía a good destination for road cycling?
A: Buendía stands out for its unique combination of natural beauty and varied terrain. Cyclists can enjoy the tranquil pine forests, the striking sandstone formations, and the expansive views of the Buendía and Entrepeñas reservoirs. The mix of flat sections and challenging climbs caters to a wide range of abilities, making it an appealing destination for diverse road cycling experiences.
tips: Viewpoint with magnificent views of the Entrepeñas dam.
Nice descent with fun slides and good views of Auñón.
Magnificent arched viaduct built over the Tagus River and the Entrepeñas Reservoir.
Puerto de Casasana with magnificent views of Pareja and surroundings.

Just 5 minutes from the town of Córcoles are the ruins of the monastery that was part of the Cistercian complex dating from the second half of the 12th century and is a clear example of the presence of this Order throughout the Iberian Peninsula. The ruins can be visited, check the official website:
https://cultura.castillalamancha.es/patrimonio/yacimientos-visitables/monasterio-de-monsalud

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike route?
faqs.answer: This challenging racebike route is estimated to take around 4 hours and 17 minutes to complete. However, your actual time may vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows paved surfaces, winding through panoramic roads. You'll experience significant elevation changes and climbs as you traverse between mountains densely covered with oaks, Pyrenean oaks, and pine forests. Expect scenic views of the reservoirs and the Tagus River.

faqs.question: Is this racebike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult and requires a very good level of fitness due to its length (~86 km) and significant elevation gain (~1243 m). It is best suited for experienced road cyclists.

faqs.question: Where does the Embalse de Entrepeñas y Puerto Casasana circular route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts and ends in Buendía. While specific parking details aren't provided, Buendía is a small town, and you should be able to find suitable parking for your vehicle near the town center to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to cycle this route are typically during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot in this region, and winter may bring colder temperatures and potentially adverse weather conditions for cycling.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several points of interest. You'll pass by the impressive View of the Entrepeñas Dam, enjoy the scenery from Madroñal Road and View of Auñón, cross the Entrepeñas Viaduct, and ascend to Puerto de Casasana. You'll also have the opportunity to see the historic Monastery of Santa María de Monsalud.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Camino Natural del Tajo and the Camino de Santiago en Guadalajara, as well as smaller segments of the Ruta de la ermita de Nuestra Señora de los Desamparados, Camino de la Isabela, and Calle de las Campanas.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de Entrepeñas y Puerto Casasana route dog-friendly for cyclists?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is on public roads, bringing a dog along for an 86 km racebike tour is generally not recommended due to the high intensity and length. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it is safely secured in a specialized bike trailer designed for pets, and always adhere to local leash laws and regulations in any towns or rest stops.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It primarily uses public roads and passes through natural areas that are freely accessible.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to refuel along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through some villages, it's a demanding ride, and services might be sparse in certain sections. It's highly recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks for the entire duration. You may find cafes or small shops in towns like Buendía at the start/end, or potentially in other small settlements you pass through, but do not rely on them for consistent refueling opportunities.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a day of cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, energy gels or snacks, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), appropriate cycling attire, sun protection (sunscreen, sunglasses), a helmet, and a fully charged phone. A GPS device or the komoot app on your phone is also highly recommended for navigation.
